title: Aave Launches GHO on Ethereum Layer 2 Networks
description: Leading decentralized lending protocol Aave has officially expanded its native stablecoin GHO to several Ethereum Layer 2 networks, marking a significant milestone in the protocol's multichain strategy.
lead: Aave has expanded its GHO stablecoin to the Arbitrum, Optimism and Polygon networks
author: Editorial

The technical implementation uses Aave's new cross-chain infrastructure, allowing GHO to maintain its overcollateralization mechanism across different networks. Users can now mint GHO against various types of collateral, including:
Interest rates are dynamically adjusted based on demand patterns specific to each network.

The Layer 2 expansion also introduces improved governance features through Aave's DAO structure. Token holders can now propose and vote on network-specific parameters, creating a more responsive monetary policy framework.
Market analysts view this development as strategically important for Aave's competitive positioning against other decentralized stablecoin projects.
The timing coincides with increased regulatory scrutiny of centralized stablecoins, potentially driving demand for decentralized alternatives with greater transparency and community governance.
This move positions Aave to capture a larger share of the
stablecoin market by giving users more cost-effective access to decentralized financial services across multiple blockchain ecosystems.
